title: A dataset recording joint EEG-fMRI during affective music listening
description: A joint EEG-fMRI dataset acquired from 21 healthy adult participants during affective music listening. Participants listened to music stimuli designed to elicit different emotional responses and provided self-reported affective state ratings. This multimodal neuroimaging dataset enables investigation of the neural correlates of emotional responses to music across both electrophysiological and hemodynamic measures.
methods_description: Data were collected using simultaneous EEG-fMRI recording from 21 healthy adult participants while they listened to music stimuli selected to produce varying affective responses. Participants self-reported their felt affective states during the listening task. The experiment and detailed acquisition parameters are described in Daly et al. (2019).
